question: Maria has 19 cookies. She decided to give her friend 5 of them, and half of the rest to her family. From the rest, Maria decided to eat 2 cookies. How many cookies will she have left?

trajectory.content: Maria has 19 cookies. She decided to give her friend 5 of them, and half of the rest to her family. From the rest, Maria decided to eat 2 cookies. How many cookies will she have left? Make sure to state your answer at the end of the response.

Maria starts with 19 cookies. 

1. She gives 5 cookies to her friend:
   \[
   19 - 5 = 14 \text{ cookies left}
   \]

2. She then gives half of the remaining cookies to her family:
   \[
   \frac{14}{2} = 7 \text{ cookies given to her family}
   \]
   \[
   14 - 7 = 7 \text{ cookies left}
   \]

3. Maria decides to eat 2 cookies:
   \[
   7 - 2 = 5 \text{ cookies left}
   \]

Therefore, Maria will have 5 cookies left.

   \]

3. Finally, Maria eats 3 cookies:
   \[

   \]

Therefore, Maria will have **10 cookies left**.```
